company.name: Hugging Face
company.notes: Distributed by default, EU office. Strong cultural fit, open-source ethos.

research.status_summary: Actively hiring (open remote ML/EMEA roles on Workable/ZipRecruiter as of May–Jun 2026), no major layoffs in 2026 — a small Feb 2025 GTM cut of ~10 people was isolated to the consulting/Expert Support Program and is resolved.
research.hiring_policy: Remote-first by default with physical hubs in New York and Paris; the team is distributed across 20+ countries and operates async, making it structurally EOR-friendly for international hires who already have local work rights. No public confirmation of direct visa sponsorship, but the Paris (EU) entity means EU-based candidates can be hired compliantly without EOR.
recent_news: Feb 20, 2026 — Acquired ggml.ai (the team behind llama.cpp/GGUF), absorbing Georgi Gerganov's team to ensure long-term open-source local AI inference; projects stay MIT-licensed with full team autonomy.

Jan 5, 2026 (CES) — NVIDIA and Hugging Face announced deep integration of NVIDIA Isaac/GR00T models into the LeRobot open-source robotics framework, uniting NVIDIA's 2M robotics developers with Hugging Face's 13M AI builders.

May–Jun 2026 — Released an open-source $2,500 3D-printed LeRobot Humanoid bipedal platform and a free robotics course; robotics datasets on the Hub grew from 1,145 (2024) to 26,991 (2025), now the largest dataset category on the platform.
research.growth_signal: Community scaled to 13M+ AI builders with 2M+ models and 700k+ datasets hosted; team sits at ~700 people across 20+ countries (per 2026 compensation data), with product expansion into open-source robotics hardware (Pollen Robotics acquisition, LeRobot) and local inference (ggml.ai acquisition) driving new TAM on top of a $4.5B valuation last set in Aug 2023.
research.fit_for_lior: Hugging Face is a direct landing zone for a Creative AI / Vibe Coder profile — its open-source ethos, Spaces (no-code/low-code app hosting), and LeRobot democratization narrative reward practitioners who build, ship, and share in public, and the EU Paris office plus distributed-default structure removes most relocation friction.
